entries.title: Salve! So it begins
entries.todayLatin: The first lesson felt straightforward. Handwriting helped the ideas settle, while pronunciation and word endings remain things to revisit through repetition.
journal: So it begins. The first lesson was quite straightforward. I am still uncertain whether my accent is correct, but I have found when learning other languages that this comes more naturally as I learn.

I tend to do all of my work digitally, and you will notice that my handwriting is terrible as a result. Putting pen to paper was refreshing, however, and I do feel I understood the lesson better because of it.
I am not sure that I have understood the endings of words yet — the -ums, the -its and the -os — but I am hoping repetition will build the concept.

entries.title: Nouns: first patterns
entries.todayLatin: This entry will be written from Alan’s notes, notebook image and reflections after Lesson II has been completed.
journal: The reusable record is prepared behind the scenes, but no public learning claim is made before the real work exists.
